info.description: Global federation server for immerle. Two faces: a PUBLIC API consumed by instances (distribution of editorial & recommendation playlists, anonymised opt-in ingestion) and a back-office ADMIN API (editorial curation, recommendations, Spotify import). Instances self-register against their owner's user id; each instance has a fixed id (a uuid, sent in X-Instance-ID) plus an editable sqid handle. All track identifiers are portable (MusicBrainz id or a normalised artist|title key) — never instance-local, never a user id. Both APIs authenticate with `Authorization: Bearer <token>`: the PUBLIC API uses a per-instance API key (the `admin-*`, `editorial`, `reco`, `spotify`, `ingest-admin` tags require the ADMIN_TOKEN instead).
info.title: immerle-hub API

get.description: The caller's dashboard: every instance they have registered.

delete.description: Permanently removes the instance and, by cascade, all of its raw submissions, then recomputes the k-anonymous aggregates.

post.description: Mints a fresh private key (returned ONCE as "privateKey"); the previous key stops working immediately. The id (uuid) is unchanged.
Creates a hub account and returns a session token (Bearer) for the dashboard.
Runs the item-item CF engine, publishes the generated playlists and returns the run with coverage/diversity metrics.
Ingests aggregated, anonymised co-play counters (track pairs heard together), in portable identifiers (opt-in instances only).

Called by an immerle instance with its owner's user id (a uuid). The hub creates the instance owned by that user, assigns its fixed id (a uuid, used in X-Instance-ID), a sqid handle (editable later) and a private key (returned ONCE). The instance then authenticates with "X-Instance-ID: <id>" and "Authorization: Bearer <privateKey>".

oneOf.description: Owner user id and optional metadata

requestBody.description: Owner user id and optional metadata

patch.description: Edits the calling instance's name, its sqid handle (must be unique hub-side) and/or its ingestion opt-in. The id (uuid) is immutable and the private key is unchanged.

delete.description: Deletes the calling instance and, by cascade, all of its raw submissions, then recomputes the k-anonymous aggregates so it no longer contributes to anything.

post.description: Subscribes the calling instance to a target identified by its id (uuid) or its sqid handle. Idempotent.
Announces the calling instance to the hub: records a heartbeat and the reported version, and returns the instance profile.

get.description: Finds active instances by exact sqid or a case-insensitive substring of the name (ILIKE). The calling instance is excluded. Returns non-sensitive summaries.

parameters.description: Exact sqid or a name fragment
parameters.name: q

get.description: Returns every published playlist (editorial + recommendation), with tracks, in portable identifiers. Supports collection-level ETag (If-None-Match) and If-Modified-Since → 304.

parameters.description: Narrow to a region (defaults to the instance's region)
parameters.name: region

post.description: Ingests aggregated, anonymised play counters in portable identifiers (opt-in instances only). Never send a user id or an instance-local track id.

Backward-compatible alias accepting the original federation client's opaque per-instance hash counters. These keys are not portable across instances, so by design they never reach cross-instance k-anonymity.

Enqueues an import of a PUBLIC Spotify playlist for the calling instance and returns a job id immediately — no Spotify call happens in this request. A background worker fetches the playlist (one at a time, honouring Spotify's rate limit). Poll GET /api/v1/spotify/imports/{id}.

get.description: Returns the job state: pending | in_progress | completed | failed. Tracks (in portable ids) are included once completed. Scoped to the calling instance.
